channel.title: Hugo's Blog
channel.description: GPU programming, functional programming, and efficient code.

item.title: My new post
item.description: I want to post my new post to look what's happpening

item.title: Monalis, stylized image compression with simulated annealing
item.description: Using webgpu compute shaders to run an highly optimized simulated annealing process to generate cool images

item.title: Wrestling with exponential compile times in GHC
item.description: How type families can easily generate exponetially large types and how to fix it.

item.title: Cracking the Cryptic (with Z3 and Rust)
item.description: Solving a crazy sudoku like puzzle using the Z3 bindings in Rust

item.title: MADS
item.description: Making a math search problem 5x as fast and then 1000x as fast

item.title: Voronoi shading the web
item.description: Creating a simple 2d shader with Rust and Bevy, and compiling it to the web

item.title: $ is the wrong way around (sometimes)
item.description: Reverse application in functional languages is underappreciated

item.title: 3d normals are 2.0000001d
item.description: How to compress a 3d normal to 2 floats for faster raytracing

item.title: Statically Typed Lambda Calculus as an Embedded Language
item.description: Using type level programming to implement statically typed lambda calculus with strong correctness guarantees

item.title: Solving newspaper puzzles with SMT solvers
item.description: Letting Z3 do the heavy lifting to solve a frustrating puzzle

item.title: Higher Kinded Types in Rust
item.description: Learning a new language: Hello World, prime numbers, then monads

item.title: Demystifying the state monad
item.description: Rediscovering haskell's state monad from scratch

item.title: Functional in the wild: typeclasses
item.description: How C++ template can be (and are) used to create typclasses

item.title: Sudoku solving
item.description: Solving sudoku's with an ILP model

item.title: Who still uses ReaderT
item.description: How haskell's ImplicitParams extension could deprecate ReaderT

item.title: Shining light on a die
item.description: Pathtracing or how I learned to stop worrying and love the nested integral

item.title: The kernel is on fire
item.description: A full tutorial on making a fire effect using a kernel on the gpu

item.title: A Hacking Bootcamp
item.description: Challenge the Cyber bootcamp 2020, a first hand account

item.title: A Boomerang Proof
item.description: Solving and proofing a children's math puzzle

item.title: The fun of playing with shaders
item.description: Why leveraging the GPU to serve your imagination is utterly enjoyable

item.title: C# can be functional pt2: Lists are Monads
item.description: An experiment to see if a list can be implemented as a monad in C#

item.title: C# can be functional pt1: The Maybe Monad
item.description: Piping the power of haskell to the world of C#: Theorists' wet dream or awesome idea?

item.title: The Knight of Hamilton
item.description: An epic tale of a struggling knight, tasked with finding a 'Knight's Tour'

item.title: Making a blog website
item.description: Creating a static html blog managed with a python CLI
